response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX embedded firmware work, Linux kernel modification, and switch/security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP). The resume shows no Japanese proficiency, no recent C development, no Linux kernel hacking, and no LAN-switch or embedded security background. The mismatch on hard-gate requirements is decisive.
strengths: Long track record of shipping production software across multiple stacks
Some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Strong testing/TDD discipline and developer-tooling mindset
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language ability — fails a stated must-have
No Linux kernel source modification or embedded firmware experience
No security-stack experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP, switch chips)
